title: salpichlaena scandens
intro: Salpichlaena scandens, also known as the Australian water fern or climbing fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dipteridaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic activities. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and aiding in the management of gastrointestinal problems. Herbal preparations of Salpichlaena scandens include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
